questionHtml:
What is the resulting concentration, in mol dm−3, when 1.0 cm3 of 0.500 mol dm−3 nitric acid solution is diluted to 50.0 cm3 with water?

A.  0.002

B.  0.01

C.  0.04

D.  0.1

meta.title: What is the resulting concentration, in mol dm−3, when 1.0 cm3 of 0.500 mol dm−3 nitric acid solution is diluted to 50.0 cm3 with water? A. 0.002 B. 0.01 C. 0.04 D. 0.1
breadcrumbLabels: Structure 1. Models of the particulate nature of matter
Structure 1.4—Counting particles by mass: The mole
Structure 1.4.5—The molar concentration is determined by the amount of solute and the volume of solution. Solve problems involving the molar concentration, amount of solute and volume of solution.
